entry.title: Re: Access Control Objects
entry.content:
In my opinion, it is DEFINE, GRANT, and WHERE.

@MappingRole: true
DEFINE ROLE role_name
{
  GRANT SELECT ON cds_entity
    WHERE access_condition_1 AND | OR
		    access_condition_2;
}

 

see the link below...

Access Control

entry.title: Re: How to Create RAP Business Events in SAP S/4HANA On Premise 2022
entry.content:
To create RAP (Restful ABAP Programming) business events in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2022:

  1. Set up your RAP environment and ensure the necessary services are active.
  2. Define the event model in your RAP business object.
  3. Use CDS views to capture and expose the relevant business data.
  4. Implement the event handling logic in ABAP, then trigger and publish the events.

Make sure to test thoroughly in your development environment before deployment.

entry.title: Avoid Overengineering in Software Development: Make it simple with KISS,DRY,YAGNI and MVP
entry.content:
Overengineering in software development is like building a Swiss Army knife when all you need is a simple screwdriver. It happens when developers create overly complex solutions for problems that could have been solved more simply. While the intentions behind overengineering might be good, such as aiming for flexibility, scalability, or robustness, it often leads to unnecessary complexity, increased costs, and slower development cycles.

I’m obviously a fan of clean code principles 🙈😅. In this blog, i will highlight the overengineering practices in SAP development and introduce you to practical strategies like DRY,KISS,YANGI, and maybe MVP that will help you avoid falling into the trap of over-engineering.

 

What Is Overengineering?

Overengineering occurs when developers build solutions that are far more complicated than required for the current problem. This could mean adding extra features, implementing complex architecture, or overgeneralizing code to cater to future needs that may never arise.

some examples from our SAP life :

  • Building overly complex custom ABAP modules when SAP S/4HANA already provides standard functionality to meet the requirement.
  • Implementing complex logic within SAP Fiori/freestyle UI5 apps when a simple Fiori Elements standard list report/object page App could deliver the required business value.

While it may seem prudent to "future-proof" the solution, this can backfire, leading to inflated development and maintenance efforts 😅.

The Hidden Cost of Overengineering

SAP implementations are often complex due to the nature of the enterprise environments they serve. SAP landscapes typically include a mix of complex integrated platforms, cloud services, and more. In such environments, developers are often tempted to create highly flexible and future-proof systems to meet all possible requirements. While this approach may seem like a safeguard against future changes, it can quickly lead to various tangible and intangible costs:

  • Increased Complexity: Complex solutions require more time to develop, test, and maintain, which can lead to budget overruns and timeline delays.
  • Reduced Agility: When a system is overly complex, it becomes harder to make changes quickly in response to new business requirements.
  • Higher Maintenance Costs: Overengineered solutions often require more effort to maintain, especially when custom code replaces standard SAP functionality.
  • Frustrated Business Stakeholders: Users may become frustrated when they see delays and costs rising while key functionalities are being deprioritized in favor of future-proofing that may never be needed.

YAGNI: You Aren't Gonna Need It

The YAGNI principle, which originated from the Extreme Programming (XP) methodology, advises developers not to implement functionality until it is absolutely necessary. It encourages a lean and focused approach, where only the most immediate and critical features are built.

In SAP projects, YAGNI can help avoid the tendency to add unnecessary complexity:

  • Avoid speculative features: SAP developers, especially when working with custom ABAP or UI5 applications, often anticipate potential future requirements. However, implementing such features too early can lead to bloated codebases. For example, designing overly generic data models or complex business logic for hypothetical use cases can complicate future enhancements.
  • Simplify development: By focusing on what is needed now, SAP teams can deliver faster and with more agility. This is particularly important in the rapidly changing enterprise environment, where business needs are often volatile. Whether it's developing custom apps using SAP Fiori or creating custom integrations in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), YAGNI encourages the team to focus on real requirements, not assumptions.
  • Maintainability: With fewer unnecessary features, the SAP landscape remains easier to maintain and upgrade over time, reducing technical debt and improving the long-term ROI of the project.

MVP: Minimum Viable Product

MVP focuses on delivering the simplest version of a product that meets the core needs of users, allowing teams to validate their ideas quickly.

In my opinion, MVP approach is ideal for testing new ideas, especially when you have a concept you want to bring to market or when you’re operating within a budget and need a quick, straightforward solution

Example: Developing a  SAP Fiori application that is intended to manage employee requests for leave. Instead of building an extensive system with complex reporting, notifications, and user roles, the MVP could focus solely on the essential features: a simple user interface for submitting leave requests and a basic admin interface for approving them. This allows for quick feedback from users, enabling the team to prioritize additional features based on real needs rather than assumptions.

DRY: Don’t Repeat Yourself

The DRY principle in my opinion is the main approach to better code refactoring since it aligns with the clean code principles that encourage avoiding code duplication by abstracting and reusing code wherever possible. Popularized by the book, The Pragmatic Programmer, the DRY principle states that, “every piece of knowledge must have a single, unambiguous, authoritative representation within a system.” Using the principle, logic, or algorithms that have certain functionality should only appear once in an application. This also includes refraining from using the same solution or approach multiple times to solve the same problem. If there are multiple solutions to a problem, try to consolidate them into one. This leads to a cleaner and more maintainable codebase.

Example: In an SAP ABAP program, if multiple reports are generating similar output formats (e.g., customer invoices), rather than duplicating the code for formatting in each report, developers can create a single reusable function module. This modular approach reduces redundancy, simplifies future updates (since changes need to be made in only one place), and helps maintain consistency across the application.

KISS: Keep It Simple, Stupid

In software development, code refactoring is an ongoing journey! In my opinion, the ideal code is the kind that’s self-explanatory and easy to understand even for a junior developer. The KISS principle advocates for simplicity in design and implementation. Solutions should be as simple as possible without unnecessary complexity.

Example: Suppose you are developing a custom SAP UI5 application for tracking sales orders. Instead of implementing multiple complicated user interfaces with various navigation paths, a KISS approach would mean creating a straightforward layout with a single page for viewing and updating orders. This not only makes it easier for users to understand but also simplifies the development and maintenance processes.

 

Practical Tips for Avoiding Overengineering :

  • Conduct Regular Requirement Reviews: Maintain open lines of communication with stakeholders to ensure that requirements are clear and current. Regularly revisit these requirements to adapt to changes in the business landscape.
  • Prioritize Core Functionality: When designing solutions, focus on the features that provide the most value to users. Defer any non-essential features to future iterations.
  • Use Prototyping: Develop prototypes to test ideas before committing to full-scale implementation. This can help validate assumptions and reduce the risk of building unnecessary complexity.
  • Emphasize Documentation: Maintain clear and concise documentation of requirements, design decisions, and code. This can help prevent miscommunication and ensure that all team members are aligned on project goals.
  • Encourage Team Collaboration: Foster a culture of collaboration within your development team, encouraging open discussions about design choices and potential complexities. This collective wisdom can help identify and eliminate over-engineering tendencies.

Conclusion

Over-engineering is a common challenge in software development, but it can be avoided by adopting principles like DRY,KISS,YANGI and MVP , my Final word is here that, continued code refactoring is part of the game but always try to keep it as simple as possible.

“So if you want to go fast, if you want to get done quickly, if you want your code to be easy to write, make it easy to read.” ― Robert C. Martin, Clean Code: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship. The Clean Code Lesson 1 by Uncle Bob 😉   

 

Happy coding 🤗 😉!!

entry.title: Data locking automatically without manual intervention in SAC Planning on specific conditions
entry.content:
There is some requirement on Data locking automatically without manual intervention in SAC Planning World,

we have a Model-1 with 100 cost centers.

1.For First week of planning all 100 cost centers to be available for planing .

2.In second week Only specifc cost centers to be locked in Model-1 based on the cost centers available in Model-2
(Model-2 contains all valid cost centers) , we need to keep valid cost centers in separate model to update from Source every week.


Multi action either can lock or unlock the entire data based on parameter of dimension only not based on property of cost center dimension.

 

Summary is like Not able to lock specific cost centers- automation is required no manual intervention.

This is quite easy using driver based functionality in BPC world.

Any leads here greatly appreciated!

Thanks in Advance Surya Harsha

entry.title: Re: GR posting error in BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content: Through standalone BAPI. Did they run with SE37? If yes, just make sure that you pass exactly the same parameters, and that's it.

entry.content:
Thanks, for invitation.

I'm excited to continue to envolve in SAP.

entry.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Using the JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
A Step-by-Step Guide to Using the JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite.

The JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite provides a robust mechanism for integrating SAP systems with external databases. Here's a step-by-step guide to establish a JDBC connection and perform data exchange.

Prerequisites:

Before you can effectively use the JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite, ensure that the following prerequisites are met:

1. SAP Integration Suite License: You need a valid SAP Integration Suite license that includes the JDBC Adapter.

2. Database Access: You must have access to the database you want to connect to, including the necessary credentials (username, password, connection URL).

3. JDBC Driver: The appropriate JDBC driver for your database must be installed and configured in the SAP Integration Suite environment.

4. Integration Suite Environment: You need an active SAP Integration Suite environment with the necessary roles and permissions to create data sources, communication channels, and integration flows.

5. Basic Knowledge of Integration Concepts: Familiarity with integration concepts, such as message flows, adapters, and mappings, is beneficial.

6. Understanding of JDBC: A basic understanding of JDBC (Java Database Connectivity) is helpful for configuring the connection and working with database-specific features.

By ensuring these prerequisites are in place, you'll be well-prepared to effectively use the JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite and integrate your SAP systems with external databases.

To connect a JDBC driver in SAP Integration Suite:

  1. Navigate: Access the Integration Suite administration console.
  2. Locate JDBC Material: Go to Monitor > Integrations and APIs > JDBC Material.
  3. Add JDBC Driver: Click on the Add button to add a new JDBC driver.

Choose the JDBC driver that aligns with your specific database requirements, such as Oracle or SQL Server.

You can download the required JDBC driver from the SAP Help portal.

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configure-jdbc-drivers

To connect a JDBC Data Source in SAP Integration Suite:

  1. Navigate: Access the Integration Suite administration console.
  2. Locate JDBC Material: Go to Monitor > Integrations and APIs > JDBC Material.
  3. Add JDBC Data Source: Click on the Add button to add a new JDBC Data Source.

IFlow:

To initiate data retrieval from a database using the JDBC adapter as a sender, you'll need to start the integration flow with a timer. The polling interval can be configured within the timer settings.

Note: The sender JDBC adapter might not be directly available in SAP Integration Suite.

Timer:

The timer purpose in the JDBC adapter within SAP Integration Suite is to control the frequency and timing of data retrieval or updates from the connected database. It allows you to schedule and automate data exchange tasks based on specific time intervals.

Scheduling: You can define the frequency at which the adapter should initiate data retrieval or update operations. This can be set in various time units, such as minutes, hours, days, or weeks.

Fetch the table data: Content modifier property details

Query:

The SELECT query should be specified in the "Query" property of the content modifier.

This ensures that the correct data is extracted and transformed based on the defined SQL statement.

Content Modifier Message Body:

${property.Query}

JDBC Adapter:

The data source name specified in the JDBC Material within the Monitoring section should be referred to as the "JDBC Data Source Alias."

This alias is used to uniquely identify and reference the data source within the integration flow configuration.

Batch Mode:

The JDBC adapter in SAP Integration Suite supports batch mode processing, which can significantly improve performance for large data volumes. Batch mode allows you to process multiple messages or data records in a single transaction, reducing the number of round trips between the SAP Integration Suite and the external database.

Limitations of Batch Mode in the JDBC Adapter in SAP Integration Suite

While batch mode in the JDBC adapter offers significant performance benefits, it's essential to be aware of its limitations:

  1. Data Volume Constraints: If you're dealing with extremely large data volumes, batch mode might still encounter performance bottlenecks. In such cases, consider alternative approaches like parallel processing or data partitioning.
  2. Transaction Rollback: If an error occurs during batch processing, the entire batch might be rolled back. This could lead to data inconsistencies or processing delays. To mitigate this, you can implement partial commit or checkpointing mechanisms within your integration flow.
  3. Complexity: Implementing batch mode can introduce additional complexity to your integration flow design. You need to carefully consider factors like batch size, interval, and error handling to ensure optimal performance and data integrity.
  4. Database Limitations: Some databases might have specific limitations or restrictions on batch processing. It's essential to consult the database documentation to understand any constraints.
  5. Performance Overhead: While batch mode can improve overall performance, there might be a slight overhead associated with managing batches and handling potential errors.

To effectively leverage batch mode and mitigate its limitations, consider the following:

  • Optimize Batch Size and Interval: Experiment with different batch size and interval settings to find the optimal configuration for your specific use case.
  • Implement Error Handling: Use appropriate error handling mechanisms to gracefully handle exceptions and prevent data loss.
  • Consider Alternative Approaches: If batch mode is not suitable for your scenario, explore other performance optimization techniques like parallel processing or data partitioning.
  • Monitor and Fine-tune: Continuously monitor the performance of your integration flow and make adjustments as needed to optimize batch processing.

By understanding these limitations and taking appropriate measures, you can effectively utilize batch mode in the JDBC adapter to enhance the performance of your SAP Integration Suite integrations.

The database response output refers to the data that is returned by the database in response to a query or request.

This output can vary widely depending on the specific query, database schema, and data stored within the database.

Following the JDBC Adapter, the integration flow can proceed to the next step based on the database response output and the defined requirements.

In many organizations, database connectivity relies on RAC Cluster databases, which typically utilize service-based connections.

RAC Cluster Database: A Comprehensive Overview

RAC (Real Application Clusters) is a high-availability, high-performance database technology developed by Oracle. It allows multiple instances of a database to run on separate nodes in a cluster, providing redundancy, scalability, and fault tolerance.

Key Features of RAC Cluster Database:

  • High Availability: RAC ensures continuous database availability by automatically failing over to a standby instance in case of a node or instance failure.
  • Scalability: It allows you to scale the database horizontally by adding more nodes to the cluster, increasing performance and capacity.
  • Load Balancing: RAC automatically distributes workload across the cluster nodes, improving performance and reducing bottlenecks.
  • Data Consistency: RAC maintains data consistency across all instances in the cluster using techniques like Fast Start Failover and Global Cache Coherency.
  • Disaster Recovery: RAC can be configured for disaster recovery by setting up a disaster recovery site with a separate RAC cluster.

Components of a RAC Cluster Database:

  • Nodes: Individual servers that run database instances.
  • Interconnect: A high-speed network that connects the nodes in the cluster.
  • Voting Disk: A shared disk that is used for voting during cluster membership changes.
  • Private Disk Groups: Shared disks that are used to store database data.
  • Cluster ware: The software that manages the cluster and ensures high availability.

Benefits of Using RAC Cluster Database:

  • Improved Performance: RAC can significantly improve database performance by distributing workload across multiple nodes.
  • Enhanced Availability: RAC provides high availability by automatically failing over to a standby instance in case of a failure.
  • Scalability: RAC allows you to scale the database horizontally to meet increasing demands.
  • Reduced Risk of Data Loss: RAC provides data redundancy and disaster recovery capabilities, minimizing the risk of data loss.
  • Simplified Management: RAC simplifies database management by automating many tasks, such as failover and load balancing.

Note: When connecting to a RAC (Real Application Clusters) database, the aforementioned process might not be successful.

Issue: com.sap.it.rt.adapter.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCException: The processing has failed due to error in creating metadatatable. : java.sql.SQLRecoverableException: IO Error: "The Network Adapter could not establish the connection"

Please refer to my other blog for a suitable solution.

Blog: Connecting to a RAC Database in SAP Integration Suite: A Step-by-Step Guide Using JDBC Adapter

Bhavani

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

I recently completed the course "Develop Applications Running on SAP BTP Using SAP HANA Cloud" and earned the SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP HANA Cloud 1.0 certification.

I am passionate about working with SAP because it significantly enhances business processes. It’s incredible how technology can drive such impactful improvements. I’m eager to continue learning and growing in this field to build a successful career.

entry.title: In QA01 print button having Inspection Instruction, we want print preview screen display.
entry.content: In QA01 after giving the data . we can see the print button having Inspection Instruction, if we click on the button, we are getting message ' The inspection instruction is printed when you leave the transaction' . what the client expecting is when we click on the button it should display 'status check warning' popup. when we click on continue button. it should show print preview option having number of copies, print preview, print buttons etc

entry.title: Re: Extract ANLZ Time-Dependent Asset Allocations Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program For Selected C
entry.content: Sorry to say that but posting a "discussion" without any question or request, whose code is reading data from a table and stores it into CSV, in which the code is almost identical to the previous post (15 posts in 2 weeks!), just the table name is changing, it's there is nothing new, no challenge, so zero interest for a blog post, zero interest for a "discussion". entry.title: Re: SAP Community Voice: Devtoberfest, New Features, Popular Content, and More
entry.content:
A quick addition: While Devtoberfest runs through October 25, Spooktoberfest runs through October 27!

Share your scary story or picture at https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/spooktoberfest-2024/m-p/13903993.

Here's my contribution: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/spooktoberfest-2024/m-p/13904919/highlight/true#M1620.

Spotted that cute fella today during our annual trip to the scarecrow festival in Peddler's Village (Lahaska, Pennsylvania, USA): https://peddlersvillage.com/event/scarecrows-in-the-village/.

Spoopy!

entry.title: error when trying to consume an orchestration with gen_ai_hub python SDK
entry.content:
When running 
service = OrchestrationService(api_url=YOUR_API_URL)
I'm getting error

TypeError: AICoreV2Client.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'base_url'

YOUR_API_URL is similar to https://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/d5...........c09

Any idea what I'm doing wrong ?

entry.title: Re: In QA01 print button having Inspection Instruction, we want print preview screen display.

entry.title: Re: In QA01 print button having Inspection Instruction, we want print preview screen display.
entry.content: I implemented EXIT_SAPLQPL1_004 but I am getting dump while executing the Tcode QA01

entry.title: Re: CAP typescript: how do I get draft data from draft en...
entry.content:
Yes, you are right: 

const draftData = await SELECT.from(SystemKinds.drafts);
is giving me all properties of my entity SystemKinds plus property "HasActiveEntity" and "DraftAdministrativeData_draftUUID" of table AdminConfigServices_SystemKinds_drafts but not the properties "HasDraftEntity" and "IsActiveEntity". But this is ok and sufficiently. 
 
I was confused because of getting error messages in VSCODE around "SystemKinds.drafts" (red underlined) . If I hover over "SystemKinds.drafts", I will get typescript error, but I get no program dumps. Thus, I thought if typescript has an issue, the program will crash... 
 
But I'm still missing typescript type definition for SystemKinds.drafts in one of the .ts files in the @CDS-models folder.
 
Yours,
Stephan

entry.title: Re: error when trying to consume an orchestration with ge...
entry.content:
My bad, I was missing to set the following env variables
AICORE_CLIENT_ID: This represents the client ID.
AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET: This stands for the client secret.
AICORE_AUTH_URL: This is the URL used to retrieve a token using the client ID and secret.
AICORE_BASE_URL: This is the URL of the service (with suffix /v2).
AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP: This represents the resource group that should be used.

entry.title: Re: error when trying to consume an orchestration with ge...
entry.content:
My bad, I was missing to set the following variables

  • AICORE_CLIENT_ID: This represents the client ID.
  • AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET: This stands for the client secret.
  • AICORE_AUTH_URL: This is the URL used to retrieve a token using the client ID and secret.
  • AICORE_BASE_URL: This is the URL of the service (with suffix /v2).
  • AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP: This represents the resource group that should be used.

entry.title: About Me
entry.content:
As a Consultant, I bring a wealth of expertise in SAP modules, including SAP SD (Sales and Distribution), CS (Customer Service), RAR (Revenue Accounting and Reporting), and TM (Transportation Management). I am a SAP Certified Application Associate specializing in SAP S/4HANA Sales, which underscores my deep understanding and proficiency in this domain.

My professional journey is marked by a strong track record in driving successful SAP implementations, providing robust support, and delivering impactful enhancements. Leveraging Agile methodologies, I ensure that projects are executed efficiently and effectively, meeting the dynamic needs of businesses.

SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Subject: Application for SAP Certification Program

Hi @David_Chaviano,

My name is Kavin, and I’m currently a college student pursuing a degree in Engineering I’m interested in the SAP certification program as I believe it will enhance my skills and improve my career prospects.

Although I’m still in school, I have a strong interest in SAP technologies and have been actively learning about ABAP and SAP Cloud Integration. I’m eager to gain hands-on experience and a certification in BTP, as I believe it will significantly boost my employability in the tech industry.

Thank you for considering my application. I’m looking forward to the opportunity to learn and grow through this program.

Best regards,
Kavinkumar

entry.title: EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed and error(s) occurred in phase MAIN_SHDIM
entry.content:
Last error code set:

Single errors (code <= 😎 found in logfile 'CP2SHDT.ELG'

Errors are accumulated in file 'CP2SHDT.ELG'

1 tp processes returned errors. View latest 'TP*.ECO' files in log-directory for

details

 

 

ERROR:
                             Detected the following errors:

# /usr/sap/trans/SUM/abap/log/SAPEBBR731.S12:
4 ETW520 already copied: "R3TRTABUTMETASTARDIMIP02"
4 ETW520 already copied: "R3TRTABUWCF_GIL_OBJECTS"
4 ETW000  [     dev trc,00000]  NTAB: version is VERS_B, UNICODELG=2, SAP_UC_LN=2
3 ETW685Xstart copying of "R3TRTABUEDOPROCSPINTDET" ...
4 ETW000 date&time: 18.10.2024 - 17:57:54
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.
2EETW000 one entry for table EDOPROCSPINTDET not copied - both insert and update failed.

 

 

Solution is given below


https://me.sap.com/notes/2354221

entry.title: i have a problem when open assetexploer (aw01n) period start from period number 6 not from period1
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @MartinRaepple,

Thank you very much for your response/support. Can you please explain how the user attribute transfer (under OpenID) works?

How are user attributes transferred to SAP BTP under OpenID (user is not present in IAS)?

I have defined attributes in IAS like this

 but when I log into SAP BTP, it looks like this:

Do the attributes need to be assigned to the app in Azure?

I don't understand how attributes are transferred under OpenID. Where should the attributes be maintained?

How can I transfer for exymple attributes like employee_id?

 

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: How can I load Report from Crystal Server 2020 in my...
entry.content:
Which .NET SDK MSI package did you install? It does need to be the BOE 2020 Server .NET runtime, you cannot use CR for Visual Studio, it can't connect to BOE/CRS

I have a sample to get you started but you should be able to log into the Support site and search the KBA system for a program I called BOESample, sorry I'm retired now so I don't have access to the systems any more to find it.

This is kind of a hack but here's the main part of my test app:

private void btnRasOpen_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    // NOTE: Cortez does not support managed reporting so have to look into using version 14 Enterprise dll's to connect to CRSE 2011.
    rptClientDoc = new ReportClientDocument();
    DateTime dtStart;
    TimeSpan difference;
    IsUnManRpt = true;

    // do not modify this works
    // this line uses the local PC as the RAS server/services running
    if (ChkLocalHost.Checked)
    {
        rptClientDoc.ReportAppServer = System.Environment.MachineName; // + ":1566";
        RASInitialized = true;
    }
    else
        rptClientDoc.ReportAppServer = btrDataFile.ToString();

    // rassdk means load from local HD
    // ras requires access to the Ras Server folder
    String RPTPath = "rassdk://"; //c:\\RASReports\\formulas.rpt"; //btnReportName.Text; // "rassdk://c:\\RASReports\\formulas.rpt"; c:\\RASReports\\formulas.rpt
    //Set the ReportAppServer and open the document

    openFileDialog.Filter = "Crystal Reports (*.rpt)|*.rpt|Crystal Reports Secure (*.rptr)|*.rptr";
    //openFileDialog1.Filter = "txt files (*.txt)|*.txt|All files (*.*)|*.*";
    openFileDialog.FilterIndex = 1;

    if (openFileDialog.ShowDialog() == DialogResult.OK)
    {
        btnCloserpt.Enabled = false;
        object rptName = openFileDialog.FileName;
        RPTPath += rptName;
        btnReportName.Text = RPTPath.ToString();
        dtStart = DateTime.Now;
    }

    object RPTObject = (object)RPTPath;
    try
    {
        dtStart = DateTime.Now;
        rptClientDoc.Open(ref RPTObject, 0);
        difference = DateTime.Now.Subtract(dtStart);
        btnReportObjects.Text += "Report Document Load: " + difference.Minutes.ToString() + ":" + difference.Seconds.ToString() + "\r\n";
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        MessageBox.Show("ERROR: " + ex.Message);
        return;
    }


    // do not modify this works
    ViewReport.Enabled = true;
    btnCloserpt.Enabled = true;
}

private void ManagedReportsListBox_SelectedIndexChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{

}

private void btnListReports_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.SessionMgr sessionMgr = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.SessionMgr();
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.EnterpriseService enterpriseService;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.EnterpriseSession enterpriseSession;

    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoObjects infoObjects;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore infoStore;

    btnReportObjects.Text += "\nConnecting to BOE Server - ";
    btnReportObjects.AppendText(btrDataFile.Text + "- getting list of Reports ****** PLEASE WAIT ******\n");
    btnReportObjects.Update();

    try
    {
        if (ChkLocalHost.Checked)
            enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.Logon(btrFileLocation.Text, btrPassword.Text, System.Environment.MachineName + ":" + CMSPort.Text, btrSearchPath.Text);
        else
            enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.Logon(btrFileLocation.Text, btrPassword.Text, btrDataFile.Text + ":" + CMSPort.Text, btrSearchPath.Text);

        enterpriseService = enterpriseSession.GetService("InfoStore");
        infoStore = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore(enterpriseService);
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        MessageBox.Show("ERROR: " + ex.Message);
        return;
    }

    btnReportObjects.Text = "Connected to BOE Server - " + btrDataFile.Text;

    enterpriseService = enterpriseSession.GetService("InfoStore");
    infoStore = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore(enterpriseService);
    infoObjects = infoStore.Query("SELECT SI_NAME, SI_ID, SI_GUID FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS WHERE SI_KIND='CrystalReport'");
    if (infoObjects.Count == 0)
        btnReportObjects.Text += "\nNo Crystal Reports in this system";
    //ceinfoObjects = infoObjects;
    int BItboxManagedRptCount = 0;

    foreach (CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoObject IObj in infoObjects)
    {
        ManagedReportsListBox.Items.Add(IObj.ID + " : " + IObj.Title.ToString());
        ++BItboxManagedRptCount;
        tboxManagedRptCount.Text = BItboxManagedRptCount.ToString();
    }

    // this line should return the number of concurrent reports being executed
    //int xxx = CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.GetConcurrentUsage();
    //infoObjects = infoStore.Query("Select * From CI_SYSTEMOBJECTS Where SI_PROGID='CrystalEnterprise.Server' and SI_DESCRIPTION='Crystal Reports 2020 Report Application Server'");
    //CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Desktop.Server currentServer = (CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Desktop.Server)infoObjects[1];
    //CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Desktop.Server svr = (CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Desktop.Server)currentServer;

    //CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Admin.ReportAppServerAdmin rptAppAdmin = (CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Admin.ReportAppServerAdmin)svr.ServerAdmin;

    //btnReportObjects.Text += "\nDoc Count: " + rptAppAdmin.CurrentDocumentCount;
    //btnReportObjects.Text += "\nMax Num Records: " + rptAppAdmin.MaxNumOfRecords;
    //btnReportObjects.Text += "\nMax Report Jobs: " + rptAppAdmin.MaxReportJobs;
    //btnReportObjects.Text += "\nN um of Browsing REcords: " + rptAppAdmin.NumOfBrowsingRecords;


    //serverMetrics.CurrentDocumentCount.ToString();
    //btnReportObjects.Text += "\nOpen Connections: " + serverMetrics.CurrentDocumentCount.ToString() + "\n";

    // now that the report ID's are listed close this session - opens a new session when report is opened
    enterpriseSession.Dispose();
}

private void btnManagedRAS_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.SessionMgr sessionMgr = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.SessionMgr();
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.EnterpriseService enterpriseService;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.EnterpriseSession enterpriseSession;

    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoObject RPTinfoObject;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoObjects RPTinfoObjects;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore infoStore;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoObjects ceInfoObjects;
    CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.Controllers.RowsetController boRowSetController;
    CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Admin.ISReportAppServerAdmin serverMetrics; // = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.Admin.ISReportAppServerAdmin();

    try
    {
        //enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.Logon("Administrator", "Password1", btrDataFile.Text, "secEnterprise");
        if (ChkLocalHost.Checked)
            enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.Logon(btrFileLocation.Text, btrPassword.Text, System.Environment.MachineName + ":" + CMSPort.Text, btrSearchPath.Text);
        else
            enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.Logon(btrFileLocation.Text, btrPassword.Text, btrDataFile.Text + ":" + CMSPort.Text, btrSearchPath.Text);

        enterpriseService = enterpriseSession.GetService("InfoStore");
        infoStore = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore(enterpriseService);
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        MessageBox.Show("Error: " + ex.Message);
        return;
    }

    ReportAppFactory Raf = (ReportAppFactory)enterpriseSession.GetService("RASReportFactory").Interface;
    DateTime dtStart;
    TimeSpan difference;

    try
    {
        dtStart = DateTime.Now;
        if (ManagedReportsListBox.SelectedItem != null)
        {
            //string MyPaperSource = cboDefaultPaperTrays.SelectedItem.ToString();
            string MySTRTemp = ManagedReportsListBox.SelectedItem.ToString();
            int MySI_ID = MySTRTemp.LastIndexOf(@":");
            int MMySTRTemp = Convert.ToInt32(ManagedReportsListBox.Text.Substring(0, MySI_ID));

            CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore istore = new CrystalDecisions.Enterprise.InfoStore(enterpriseSession.GetService("InfoStore"));
            //Query CMS for the report.
            ceInfoObjects = istore.Query("SELECT * FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS WHERE SI_ID=" + MMySTRTemp);

            // now open the report using Engine from FRS to get various properties
            RPTinfoObjects = infoStore.Query("SELECT * FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS WHERE SI_KIND='CrystalReport' and SI_ID=" + MMySTRTemp.ToString());
            RPTinfoObject = RPTinfoObjects[1];

            // Managed
            rptClientDocMgd = Raf.OpenDocument(MMySTRTemp, 0);

            btnReportName.Text = "CUID - " + MySTRTemp;

            difference = DateTime.Now.Subtract(dtStart);
            btnReportObjects.Text += "Report Document Load: " + difference.Minutes.ToString() + ":" + difference.Seconds.ToString() + "\r\n";
            btnCloserpt.Enabled = false;

            dtStart = DateTime.Now;
            ViewReport.Enabled = true;
            btnCloserpt.Enabled = true;

            cbLastSaveHistory.Text = "";
            try
            {
                // Read File Details from CFileInfo Object
                cbLastSaveHistory.Sorted = true;

                for (int x = 0; x < rptClientDocMgd.HistoryInfos.Count; x++)
                {
                    cbLastSaveHistory.Items.Add(rptClientDocMgd.HistoryInfos[x].BuildVersion.ToString() + ": Date: " + rptClientDocMgd.HistoryInfos[x].SavedDate.ToString());
                }
                cbLastSaveHistory.SelectedIndex = 0; // rpt.HistoryInfos.Count - 1;

                //SP 13
                //•	RAS .NET SDK 
                //ReportClientDocument.HistoryInfos[i].SavedDate
                //ReportClientDocument.HistoryInfos[i].BuildVersion

                //•	CR .NET SDK 
                //ReportDocument.HistoryInfos[i].SavedDate
                //ReportDocument.HistoryInfos[i].BuildVersion
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                //MessageBox.Show("Error: " + ex.Message);
                //MessageBox.Show("This report has no Save history");
                cbLastSaveHistory.Text = "This report has no Save history";
            }

            boRowSetController = rptClientDocMgd.RowsetController;
            CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.DataDefModel.GroupPath gp = new CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.DataDefModel.GroupPath();
            string tmp = String.Empty;
            try
            {
                rptClientDocMgd.RowsetController.GetSQLStatement(gp, out tmp);
                btnReportObjects.Text = tmp;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                btnReportObjects.Text = "ERROR: " + ex.Message;
                return;
            }

        }
        else
        {

        }
        //btnReportObjects.Text = xrd.ToString();
        //btnReportObjects.Update();
        //rptClientDocMgd.Close();
        return;
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        if (ex.Message == "Cannot logon with the custom database information specified in the InfoStore.  This is a configuration problem. Please contact your system administrator.")
        {
            MessageBox.Show("ReportLogonMode: Use user database credentials for database logon.\nThe report requires User permissions set up in the Database before it can be opened.");
            btnReportObjects.Clear();
            return;
        }
        if (ex.Message == "Unsupported Operation. A document processed by the JRC engine cannot be opened in the C++ stack.")
            MessageBox.Show("That is a Crystal Reports for Enterprise report, you cannot open it in a .NET application");
        else
            MessageBox.Show("ERROR: " + ex.Message);
        return;
    }
}

private void timeCounter(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    DateTime dtStart;
    dtStart = DateTime.Now;
    btnReportObjects.Text += "\nClicked View Report End time: \n" + dtStart.ToString();

    string[] dateValues = { "30/12/2011", "12/30/2011", "30/12/11", "12/30/11" };
    string pattern = "MM/dd/yyyy";
    DateTime parsedDate;

    foreach (var dateValue in dateValues)
    {
        if (DateTime.TryParseExact(dateValue, pattern, null, DateTimeStyles.None, out parsedDate))
            MessageBox.Show("Converted: " + dateValue + " to: " + parsedDate);
        else
            MessageBox.Show("Unable to convert: " + dateValue + " to a date and time.");
    }
}

private void Closerpt_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    btnManagedRAS.Enabled = true;
    btnCloserpt.Enabled = false;
    ViewReport.Enabled = false;
    btnReportName.Text = "Select CeLocale before opening a report";
    btnReportObjects.Text = "";
    crystalReportViewer2.SetProductLocale(1033);

    try
    {
        if (IsUnManRpt)
        {
            rptClientDoc.Close();
            IsUnManRpt = false;
        }
        else
        {
            if (rptClientDocMgd != null)
                rptClientDocMgd.Close();
        }
    }
    catch { }

    crystalReportViewer2.ReportSource = null;
    if (!crystalReportViewer2.Disposing)
        btnReportObjects.Text += "Viewer is disposing";
    else
        btnReportObjects.Text += "Viewer is disposed";
            
    crystalReportViewer2.Refresh();
    GC.Collect();

    ManagedReportsListBox.Items.Clear();
    ManagedReportsListBox.Text = "";
    tboxManagedRptCount.Text = "";

    try
    {
        MyUNIApp.Application.Quit();
    }
    catch { }
}

Note you create a session to BOE/CRS Server and then get a list of reports buy ID and then it open/loads the report, all using RAS

entry.title: Re: Database Logon issue with Microsoft OLEDB Driver 19 f...
entry.content:
Firstly you need to upgrade CR for VS to the latest SP, currently it's SP 36

https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

Here's a blog on helping you upgrade your app:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

And see the Platforms info:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

Note: SP 17 doesn't support your SQL Server or Client.

Once you upgrade your application to SP 36 it should all start working.

Note also MS ODBC 19 is not listed as being support yet, you'll have to use 18 for now.

If you don't own the application then contact the makers for an updated version.

entry.title: Re: CR for Visual Studio SP36 32-bit (CRRuntime_32bit_13_...
entry.content:
There should be an Install log somewhere if not the search MS's KBA for Install logging using MSIEXEC.

The log should tell you what's missing, 1904 is a common error when dependencies are missing.

Be sure to be logged in as a local PC Admin account, it's required for permissions to register dll's etc.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports for Visual Studio not showing control...
entry.content:
MS ended life for VS 2015 in 2020.
CR for only supports 4.7 and 4.8 framework now, CR follows MS end of life.

I had a look at my VS 2022 and saw the same thing due to Interopt was not set to false for the ClientDoc.

Check your error list for the interopt.

See my Blog on upgrading also:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

This is what you should see now:

You do need to view your main form where the viewer is to be able to see the CR stuff.

As for 2015, see if you can add the 4.7 and 4.8 Framework to it, if not you'll have to upgrade VS.

See the platform info for more details:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

entry.title: Re: How can I load Report from Crystal Server 2020 in my...
entry.content:
Thanks, Will for your valuable response.

I'm using 'CR for Visual Studio SP36 64b installer (VS 2022 and above)', 'CR for Visual Studio SP36 CR Runtime 64-bit MSI'.

Downloaded from SAP site : https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

Yes, you just mentioned it should not be run time. Could you suggest me from where can I download 'BOE 2020 Server .NET runtime'? is it separate extension for Visual Studio? 

Searching over all blog, to connect to Crystal Server from .NET everyone refers to above installer. and mentioned few examples like below. Eaven though But I'm not able to load the reports from reports server 2020.

Example 1: 

string connectionString = "http://your-server-address:port/BOE/OpenDocument/lus/bwd/report/your_report_name";

ReportDocument reportDoc = new ReportDocument();

reportDoc.Load(connectionString);

Example 2: 

// Create a connection service object
ConnectionService connectionService = new ConnectionService();

// Set the connection string
connectionService.SetConnection("YourCrystalServerConnectionString");

// Open the report document
ReportDocument reportDoc = new ReportDocument();

// Load the report from the Crystal Server
reportDoc.Load(connectionService, "YourReportName", "YourReportPath");

But using above, I always get the error at   .Load()  method. like class not registered etc.. Exactly don't know which I should mention for Report path, and connection string of the report server.  Or I'm I doing entirely wrong.  

In SSRS, all we used to do is using report Viewer control in .net and just mention required report server path while using report viewer and .rdl file name etc..  to load the report.

Please suggest.

My Requirement is simple. 

from my .NET(Visual Studio 2022), I should connect to Crystal Server 2020 where I have uploaded one report from BI Launch pad and load the report. or get that report in pdf format from Crystal server and show in .NET app.

Just confused which physical path of the report and report server path to be mentioned. 

Could you suggest me from where can I download 'BOE 2020 Server .NET runtime'?  and how to connect to the server and load the report?

Please send me sample code:

I'm doing POC and your help is valuable for my POC.

Thanks,

Karuna

entry.title: What is the best structure for organizing integration classes via PI/PO proxy?
entry.content:
I have a task to check the organization of PI classes in an environment.

The classes generated for proxy interfaces are organized as subclasses of the same class. So, when a new interface is created, instead of a new class being created for this interface, the same class destined for that namespace is used, and then only one new method is created, meaning that some objects can be reused.

This is generating several problems in the environment, because when two developers need to work at the same time on two different interfaces, a dependency occurs and one of them cannot proceed.

The choice to do the architecture this way was made a long time ago, and I imagine it will take a lot of work to undo it. However, I would like to know, in your opinion, what is the best way to organize these classes?

I usually make a class for each interface, but I'm open to ideas.

entry.title: Re: Phone.pe Wrong. Transaction. Refund. Money

entry.title: Re: New Check for App Availability in SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Readiness Check fo
entry.content:
We recently executed the SAP Readiness Check in preparation for a S/4 2020 to S/4 2023 FPS02 upgrade. The Application Availability report flagged as "Unavailable" multiple transactions that chances are will not be unavailable, like DBACOCKPIT, SE16N, SM20N, SM37, SM59, ST22, and many more.

What is the recommendation to confirm the list of applications flagged indeed will be unavailable or not?

Best regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Performance comparison between Code Pushdown Technique vs Traditional ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @everyone,

 

Case 1 - When we use code pushdown technique and pushed where conditions to CDS along with Parameterized CDS

Case 2 - When we use Parameterized CDS but didn't pushed where conditions to CDS

Case 3 - When we use normal CDS view with pushing where condition to CDS ( All filtering happened on Application layer )

In the above mentioned three cases here are the observations -

  • In this scenario I have utilized code pushdown technique and pushed all where condition in CDS and also I am utilizing Parameterized CDS to narrow down data from Database itself but in this scenario when I ran SQL Traces ( Case 1 ) it took more time in execution than the scenario where I just utilized parameterized CDS without any where condition ( Case 2 ) and the fastest execution happened when I utilized normal CDS ( Case 3 )

I have some open question related to CDS performance tuning as below -

  1. Is Code pushdown ( with Parameter and where ) slower than application layer filtering
  2. if not then how can we decide when to use parameterized CDS or when to use normal
  3. is where on CDS taking more time than application layer filtering

Looking forward to get the correct understanding.. @Andre_Fischer , @thomas_jung , @RichHeilman 

Regards,

Harshit
